folio.label: 191r
folio.l: Heere moun men se , that dreemes be to drede
And certes in the same book , I rede
Rigút in the next chapitre , after this
I gabbe nat , so haue I ioye or blys
¢ Two men , æ wolde han passed ouä the see
For certeyn cause , in to fer contree
If æ the wynd , ne had be contrarie
That made úe~ in a Citee , for to tarie
That stood ful merye , vp oon an hauene syde
But on a day , a geyn the Eue tyde
The wynd gan chaunge , & blew rigút as úe~ leste
Iolyf and glad , they went vn to here reste
And casten úe~ ful erly , for to seyle
But to that oo man , fel a gret merveille
¢ That oon of hem , in slepynge as he lay
Hi~ mette a wonder dreem , a geyn the day
Hi~ æougút a man stood , by his beddes syde
And úi~ comaunded , that he shulde a byde
And seide úi~ thus , if æè to morwe wende
Thow shalt be dreynt , my tale is at an ende
He wook & told his felawe , what he mette
And preyed úi~ , his viage to lette
As for that day , he preyed úi~ to abyde
His felawe that lay , by his beddes syde
Gan for to laugúe , & skorned úi~ ful faste
No dreem q¶ he , may so myn herte agaste
That I wol lette , for to do myne thynges´
I sette nat a strawe , by my dremynges´
For sweuenes ben but vanitees & iapes
Men dreme alday , of Oules & of Apes
And eke of many a mase , ther w al
Men dreme of thyngö , æ neuäe was ne shal
But sithe I se , æ æè wolt heere abide
And thus forsleuthen , wilfully thy tyde
God woot it rewetú me , and haue good day
And thus he took his leue , & went his way
But or that he , had half his cours I seyled
Noot I nat why , ne what meschaunce it eyled
But casuely , the shippes bottum rente
And ship~ & man , vnder the water wente
In sigút of othere shippes , it by syde
That w hem seyled , at the same tyde
And æäfore , faire Partelote so deere

note.notetxt: 285 the last three lines look crammed. Double check.ODR
